headline: Dollar Tree Soars 17%, Dollar General Jumps 5%: Here’s Why Discount Store Stocks Are Catching a Bid
summary: Shares of Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) are up 17% in early trading Thursday, jumping from $95.87 to roughly $112 after the discount retailer posted a sizable Q1 FY2026 beat and raised its full-year profit outlook. Peer Dollar General (NYSE:DG) is riding the sympathy wave, with DG stock up 5% ahead of its own earnings report on ... Dollar Tree Soars 17%, Dollar General Jumps 5%: Here’s Why Discount Store Stocks Are Catching a Bid

headline: Dollar Tree Stock Surges After Earnings. The Retailer Has a Big New Delivery Partner.
summary: The company posted adjusted earnings of $1.74 a share for its fiscal first quarter, up from $1.26 last year and above analysts’ consensus estimate of $1.53, according to FactSet.  Earlier Thursday, delivery platform  DoorDash  announced a partnership with  Dollar Tree  to offer on-demand delivery from the retailer’s U.S. stores.
